nodes.name: NT
nodes.summary: NT表示 IUCN 近危等级，当前关联914种鸟类，如帝企鹅、小斑几维鸟、大美洲鸵等914种鸟类。

nodes.name: 果鳩屬
nodes.summary: 果鳩屬（Ptilinopus）是图谱中的分类级分类节点，当前关联52种鸟类，如Scarlet-breasted fruit dove、Wompoo fruit dove、Yellow-breasted fruit dove等52种鸟类。

nodes.name: 鸠鸽科
nodes.summary: 鸠鸽科（Columbidae）是图谱中的科级分类节点，当前关联299种鸟类，如Purple-winged ground dove、Moreno's ground dove、Black-winged ground dove等299种鸟类。

nodes.name: 鸟纲
nodes.summary: 鸟纲（Aves）是图谱中的分类级分类节点，当前关联10767种鸟类，如丹顶鹤、朱鹮、中华秋沙鸭等10767种鸟类。

nodes.name: 栖息地破坏
nodes.summary: 开发、采伐和围垦等活动导致适宜生境减少。

nodes.name: 鸽形目
nodes.summary: 鸽形目（Columbiformes）是图谱中的目级分类节点，当前关联299种鸟类，如Purple-winged ground dove、Moreno's ground dove、Black-winged ground dove等299种鸟类。

nodes.name: 脊索动物门
nodes.summary: 脊索动物门（Chordata）是图谱中的分类级分类节点，当前关联10767种鸟类，如丹顶鹤、朱鹮、中华秋沙鸭等10767种鸟类。

nodes.name: 黃頭果鳩
nodes.summary: 黃頭果鳩（Ptilinopus layardi）是图谱中的分类级分类节点，当前关联1种鸟类，如Whistling dove。

nodes.name: 动物界
nodes.summary: 动物界（Animalia）是图谱中的分类级分类节点，当前关联10767种鸟类，如丹顶鹤、朱鹮、中华秋沙鸭等10767种鸟类。

nodes.name: Whistling dove
nodes.summary: The whistling dove (Ptilinopus layardi), also known as the whistling fruit dove, velvet dove or yellow-headed dove, is a small fruit dove from Fiji. The species is endemic to the islands of Kadavu and Ono in the Kadavu Group in the south of Fiji. Taxonomy The whistling dove is the most primitive of the "golden doves", a small subgroup of the genus Ptilinopus which includes two other small Fijian fruit doves, the golden fruit dove and the orange fruit dove. The group was once split into its own genus, Chrysoenas. Description The whistling dove is a small dove (20 cm (7.9 in)) that is sexually dimorphic in its velvety plumage. The plumage of the male is dark green with a yellow head and undertail coverts, the female lacks the yellow plumage. They are difficult to see in the forest canopy, but can be found due to their distinctive call, a clear rising whistle followed by a falling 'tinkle'. Behaviour Feeding The species feeds on fruits in the forest canopy. Breeding The breeding of this species has not been studied much. A nest described in 1982 was a 'loose thin platform' constructed with twig-like vines 3 m (9.8 ft) above the ground. A single nestling was described. Only the female took care of the young. If this pattern of parental care is widespread in the golden dove group to which the whistling dove belongs, it represents an unusual adaption within the pigeon family. This difference in the levels of parental care was suggested as an explanation of the sexual dimorphism in the golden doves. Conservation The whistling dove is considered near threatened by the IUCN. The species is currently common in the forests of Kadavu and Ono, with an estimated population of 10,000 birds. However it has a restricted range and it is declining due to habitat loss. References
